english: 500 000 Chinese Glr.; _bum-p'rág ('bum phrag) yčig (gcig) a hundred thousand; _búm-tso ('bum tsho) drug (drug) 600000; mgur-_búm (mgur 'bum) the 100 000 songs, v. mgúr-ma (mgur ma).

tomb, sepulchre Cs., sku-_búm (sku 'bum), gduń-_búm (gdung 'bum) Cs., id.; sku-_búm (sku 'bum) (*kum-bum* (*kum bum*)) n.p., a large monastery on the Chinese frontier, v. Huc, also Köpp., who traces the name back to the preceding word.

I. vb. 1. to rise, to be prominent, sbáń-la (sbang la) brág-ri (brag ri) _búr-ba ('bur ba) čig (cig) a rocky hill rising from the green-sward Mil.; _búr-du ('bur du) dód-pa (dod pa) v. dód-pa (dod pa); _búr-du ('bur du) rkó-ba (rko ba) to emboss, to work out relievos Glr.; *_bur-kó gyáb-pa* (*'bur rko rgyab pa*) C., *_búr-la tón-čè* (*'bur la thon ce*) W. id. - 2. to spring up, come forth, bud, unfold, *ńo bur dug* (*ngo 'bur dug*) it is getting green W. - 3. to increase, augment, *ńo kyé-na o-ma bur dug* (*ngo skye na 'o ma 'bur dug*) when the fields are getting green, milk becomes more plentiful W. - kyoń-_bur (skyong 'bur) gold and silver ornaments in relievo on some other metal. - glo-_búr (glo 'bur), blo-_bur (blo 'bur) seems to be a technical term for some part of a building Glr. - bris-_búr (bris 'bur) paintings and sculptures. - _búr-rko-mk'an ('bur rko mkhan), _búr-bzo-pa ('bur bzo pa) engraver. - _búr-sku ('bur sku) relief-picture - _bur-rgód ('bur rgod) (s.l.c.) Ld.-Glr., Schl. 17, b., mentioned among various musical instruments (?). - _bur-jòms ('bur 'joms) with byéd-pa (byed pa) to reduce elevations, to smooth uneven ground; fig. Mil., to prostrate an opponent in disputation. - _búr-po ('bur po) 1. Sch.: projecting, prominent; a protuberance, tumor, rús-pai (rus pa'i) _búr-poi ('bur po'i) ldebs (ldebs) near the protuberance of the bone Med. 2. having protuberances, uneven, rough, opp. to _jám-po ('jam po), of the skin Med. - _búr-ma ('bur ma) embossment, relievo - II. sbst. protuberance, e g. a boil, pustule etc.

vb., pf. imp. p'ul (phul), fut. dbul (dbul) (*ul, ul* (*ul, ul*)), W. *p'úl-cè* (*phul ce*) 1. to give, when the person receiving is considered to be of higher rank (cf. gnáń-ba (gnang ba)), či (ci) tsam żig (zhig) dbúl-bar (dbul bar) bgyi (bgyi) how much shall we give you? Feer Introd. p. 70, 18; to bring in, e.g. to place a criminal before the king Dzl.; gar dań (gar dang) rtséd-mo (rtsed mo) rgyál-po-la (rgyal po la) _búl-ba ('bul ba) to perform dances etc. before the king Dzl.; gtsúg-lag-k'ań (gtsug lag khang) rgyál-po-la (rgyal po la) gzigs-par (gzigs par) _búl-ba ('bul ba) to show the king the convent-temple Glr.; to lay before, represent, report, like gsól-ba (gsol ba), tsul (tshul) rgyas (rgyas) p'úl-bas (phul bas) as they had given him a minute report of the manner in which... Mil.; p'ul (phul) żig (zhig) communicate it to me Mil.; _búl-bar ('bul bar) p'ul-nas (phul nas) Mil., prob. proposing to give, offering; lam (lam) to put a person in the way of, to put in a condition, to enable Mil.; specifically in dating letters: dkar-mdáńs-nas (dkar mdangs nas) p'ul (phul) given at Kardang. - 2. to add (arith.) Wdk. II. sbst. offering, gift, present, _búl-ba ('bul ba) mań-po (mang po) p'ul (phul) Mil., also byéd-pa (byed pa) Pth.
Sch. a coppice of young trees.

(*bé-da* (*be da*)), a class of itinerant musicians, cf. mon (mon) W. (This seems not to be a Tibetan word, but to belong to one of the mountain dialects; its spelling also - acc. to Ld.-Glr., Schl. 25, b. p. 15 - may be wrong).

Pth., *bem* (*bem*) W., C., 1. aim, goal, target; _ben ('ben) dzúgs-pa (dzugs pa) to set up a target; _bén-la ('ben la) ytod-pa (gtod pa) to aim, to take aim; _bén-sa ('ben sa) the place where the target is to be set up; specifically: the central part of the target, the mark. - 2. scope Cs. - 3. putrefaction Sch., = _bam ('bam).

pf. páb (phab), fut. dbab (dbab), imp. pob (phob) W. *p'áb-cè* (*phab ce*), causative to _báb-pa ('bab pa) 1. to cast down, throw down, ltó-ba (lto ba) sa-la (sa la) to cast one's self on the ground Dzl.; sa-rdúl (sa rdul) _bebs ('bebs) bcùg (bcug) he made (the pigeon) throw down dust Glr.; to cause to rain (e.g. jewels) frq.; kyeu (khye'u) ču (chu) _bébs-kyi ('bebs kyi) ri-mo (ri mo) a picture representing two youths who, driven by piety, conveyed by means of an elephant skins filled with water to the fishes in a dried-up pool Glr.; mig (mig) sna-rtsér (sna rtser) to keep one's eyes directed towards the tip of the nose. - 2. to subject Dzl. 2S°, 12. - 3. to put off, to lay aside, e.g. bag l. - 4. used in a variety of phrases: ynas (gnas) _bébs-pa ('bebs pa) W. *żi p'áb-cè* (*zhi phab ce*) to take up one's residence in a place; dpya (dpya) _bébs-pa ('bebs pa), with la (la), to impose
